title: Genghis Khan dies
subtitle: Founder and first khan of the Mongol Empire
fact: Genghis Khan, also known as Chinggis Khan, was the founder and first khan of the Mongol Empire.

title: Alī ibn Abī Ṭālib dies
fact: Ali ibn Abi Talib was the fourth Rashidun caliph who ruled from 656 CE until his assassination in 661, as well as the first Shia Imam.

title: Joan of Arc dies
fact: Joan of Arc is a patron saint of France, honored as a defender of the French nation for her role in the siege of Orléans and her insistence on the coronation of Charles VII of France during the Hundred Years' War.

title: Richard III of England dies
fact: Richard III was King of England from 26 June 1483 until his death in 1485.

title: Charlemagne dies
subtitle: King of Franks, regarded as the first Holy Roman Emperor
fact: Charlemagne was King of the Franks from 768, King of the Lombards from 774, and Emperor of what is now known as the Carolingian Empire from 800.

title: William the Conqueror dies
fact: William the Conqueror, sometimes called William the Bastard, was the first Norman king of England, reigning from 1066 until his death.

title: Henry VI of England dies
fact: Henry VI was King of England from 1422 to 1461 and again from 1470 to 1471, and claimant to the French throne from 1422 to 1453 under the terms of the Treaty of Troyes.

title: Richard I of England dies
fact: Richard I, known as Richard the Lionheart or Richard Cœur de Lion because of his reputation as a great military leader and warrior, was King of England from 1189 until his death in 1199.

title: Alfred the Great dies
subtitle: King of Wessex
fact: Alfred the Great was King of the West Saxons from 871 to 886, and King of the Anglo-Saxons from 886 until his death in 899.

title: John, King of England dies
fact: John was King of England from 1199 until his death in 1216.

title: Henry II of England dies
fact: Henry II was King of England from 1154 until his death in 1189.

title: Edward IV of England dies
fact: Edward IV was King of England from 4 March 1461 to 3 October 1470, then again from 11 April 1471 until his death in 1483.

title: Umar ibn Al-Khattāb dies
fact: Umar ibn al-Khattab, also spelled Omar, was the second Rashidun caliph, ruling from August 634 until his assassination in 644.

title: Thomas Aquinas dies
subtitle: Italian Dominican friar, philosopher, Catholic priest, and Doctor of the Church
fact: Thomas Aquinas was an Italian Dominican friar and priest, theologian, and philosopher.

title: Edward III of England dies
fact: Edward III, also known as Edward of Windsor before accession, was King of England from January 1327 until his death in 1377.

title: Edward II of England dies
fact: Edward II, also known as Edward of Caernarfon or Caernarvon, was King of England from 1307 until he was deposed in January 1327.

title: Rumi dies
subtitle: Iranian poet
fact: Jalāl al-Dīn Muḥammad Rūmī, commonly known as Rumi, was a Sufi mystic, poet, and founder of the Islamic brotherhood known as the Mevlevi Order.

title: Edward I of England dies
fact: Edward I, also known as Edward Longshanks and the Hammer of the Scots, was King of England from 1272 to 1307.

title: Mehmed II dies
subtitle: Seventh Sultan of the Ottoman Empire
fact: Mehmed II, commonly known as Mehmed the Conqueror, was the sultan of the Ottoman Empire twice, from August 1444 to September 1446 and then later from February 1451 to May 1481.

title: Dante Alighieri dies
subtitle: Florentine poet, writer, and philosopher
fact: Dante Alighieri, widely known mononymously as Dante, was an Italian poet, writer, and philosopher.

title: Hasan ibn Ali dies
fact: Hasan ibn Ali was an Alid political and religious leader.

title: Marco Polo dies
subtitle: Venetian explorer and merchant who travelled through Asia
fact: Marco Polo was a Venetian merchant, explorer, and writer who travelled through Asia along the Silk Road between 1271 and 1295.

title: Henry III of England dies
fact: Henry III, also known as Henry of Winchester, was King of England, Lord of Ireland and Duke of Aquitaine from 1216 until his death in 1272.

title: Cnut the Great dies
subtitle: King of Denmark, Norway, and England
fact: Cnut, also known as Canute and with the epithet the Great, was King of England from 1016, King of Denmark from 1018, and King of Norway from 1028 until his death in 1035.

title: Hildegard of Bingen dies
subtitle: German Benedictine abbess, polymath, mystic and Doctor of Church
fact: Hildegard of Bingen OSB, also known as the Sibyl of the Rhine, was a German Benedictine abbess and polymath active as a writer, composer, philosopher, mystic, visionary, and as a medical writer and practitioner of the Catholic Church during the High Middle Ages.

title: Leif Erikson dies
subtitle: Norse explorer
fact: Leif Erikson, also known as Leif the Lucky, was a Norse explorer who is thought to have been the first European to set foot on continental America, approximately half a millennium before Christopher Columbus.

title: Avicenna dies
subtitle: Persian polymath, physician and philosopher
fact: Ibn Sina, commonly known in the West as Avicenna, was a preeminent philosopher and physician of the Muslim world.
